Hot Wheels

From the Super Mario Wiki, the Mario encyclopedia
Jump to navigationJump to search
It has been suggested that this article be split into Hot Wheels, Hot Wheels Mario Kart. (discuss)

It has been requested that more images be uploaded for this article. Remove this notice only after the additional images have been added. Specifics: All figurines

Hot Wheels's logo

Hot Wheels is a brand used by the United States–based toy company Mattel introduced in 1968 that features die-cast toy cars.

Monster Jam[edit]

Donkey Kong Monster Truck[edit]

The first merchandise directly based on the Super Mario franchise produced by Hot Wheels toys were various recreations of the real-life Donkey Kong monster truck.

  • 1:24 scale diecast

  • 1:43 scale diecast

  • 1:64 scale diecast

Monster Jam (Demolition) series[edit]

  • Donkey Kong

  • Bowser

  • Double Pack

Monster Trucks series[edit]

  • Mario

  • Luigi

  • Yoshi

  • Bowser

Pop Culture[edit]

Six cars were produced in 2015 under the Pop Culture series for the 30th anniversary of the Super Mario franchise featuring artwork from 8-bit and 16-bit games of the Super Mario franchise.[1]

  • Super Mario Bros. Dairy Delivery

  • Super Mario Bros. 2 Cool-One

  • Super Mario Bros. 3 School Busted

  • Super Mario World Volkswagen T1 Panel Bus

  • Super Mario Kart A-OK

  • Dr. Mario 8 Crate Delivery

Character Cars[edit]

In August 2016, six Character Cars based on various main characters from the Super Mario franchise were released.[2][3] In 2018, a seventh Character Car based on Donkey Kong was released in addition to the original six. In 2020, a Character Car based on Bullet Bill was released, followed by Character Cars based on Light-Blue Yoshi and Princess Daisy releasing in 2021.

  • Mario character car

8-Car Series[edit]

In September 2016, eight cars featuring artwork of various Super Mario–franchise characters were released exclusively at Walmart stores.[4][3]

  • Toad Vandetta
  • Luigi Ryura LX
  • Wario RD-08
  • Bowser Cruise Bruiser
  • Princess Peach Bully Goat
  • Mario Bread Box
  • Yoshi Flathead Fury
  • Donkey Kong Super Van

Hot Wheels Mainline[edit]

  • Cool-One (2016)

  • Standard Kart (2021)

Mario Kart Series[edit]

First released in July 2019, the Mario Kart Series is mostly faithful to the game designs. The karts are metal casted while the characters are plastic sculptures based mostly on their appearance in Mario Kart 8 but are not interchangeable. In 2020, a new line of karts with gliders launched alongside the original line. These models feature a grip situated on the back part of the car that normal models do not have. As of September 2022, there is a total of 25 characters (32 counting recolors but not the Yoshi Egg Series) and 13 karts in different combinations.

Key Available as kart Available as glider Available as kart and glider

Karts[edit]

Available mostly as single packs released in wave series of between 5 and 8 characters. Most series feature 1 or 2 new characters or vehicles along with repeats of previous releases.

SDCC 2019 exclusives[edit]

Before the line was officially launched, two kart and glider models were available exclusively to the San Diego Comic-Con attendees. These exclusives came packaged in a black blind box with a case and a Rainbow Road background motif. The standard content was a Silver Mario with a Silver Standard Kart and Silver Glider (which resembles Metal Mario on his standard kart and his standard glider), but there was a 1-in-10 chance of getting a Gold version. These models have never been made commercially available.

  • Silver Mario

  • Gold Mario

SDCC 2022 exclusive[edit]

Pink Gold Peach in a Standard Kart was announced as both an in expo purchase and an online store exclusive offer for the 2022 expo. The box included was designed to reminiscent of perfume and cosmetics boxes.

  • Pink Gold Peach

SDCC 2025 exclusive[edit]

An exclusive figure of Pauline in the Badwagon was announced for SDCC 2025. This exclusive figure comes packaged in a red box that has two white stripes on the bottom, Pauline's name and emblem printed on the front, and a Moonview Highway background on the inside.

  • Pauline SDCC 2025

  • Pauline SDCC 2025

The Super Mario Bros. Movie[edit]

A selection of separately branded Hot Wheels products based on The Super Mario Bros. Movie was released in 2023.

  • Donkey Kong in his vehicle

  • Toad in his monster truck

  • Mario in his kart

  • 4-pack featuring Princess Peach's first appearance

  • Koopa Troopa and his vehicle

  • Super Mario Bros. Plumbing's plumber van

  • Jungle Kingdom Raceway Track Set, featuring the Mario die-cast
